titleStmt.title: Letter from Hoey & Denning to Sir Robert Chalmers, 10 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Hoey and Denning

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOEY & DENNING.
SOLICITORS.
DUBLIN AND TULLAMORE. TELEPHONE NOS. DUBLIN. 1478.
TULLAMORE. 5. TELEGRAPHIC ADDRESS:-
ADVOCATE DUBLIN. 12. Trinity Street.
Dublin. 10th May 1916 The Right Hon,
Sir Robert Chalmers K.C.B.,
Under Secretary for Ireland,
Dublin Castle.
Dublin. Sir, We beg to inform you that we are the family Solicitors for the
Count and Countess Plunket, who are at present in prison in Mountjoy
Prison. The Military authorities very courteously allowed a member
of our Firm to interview Countess Plunkett at Mountjoy to-day. We
understand our Clients are Suspects, as being connected with the recent
Insurrection but that they have not yet been brought to trial. As you can readily understand we are very anxious to do every-
thing we possibly can to assist our Clients at the forthcoming trial
and we would therefore be much obliged if you will let us know whether
we can instruct Counsel to appear on their behalf or, failing that
whether we can appear ourselves in defence of our Clients. We do not know whether you are the proper authority to approach
with reference to the matter but if not we would be very much obliged
indeed if you will kindly forward this letter to the proper department
and put us in touch with them. We have the honor to be,
Your Obedient Servants,
Denning
